text: where they lived all things were older than man and they hummed of mystery. ‘The first great masterpiece of the globally warmed generation. Here is an American classic which, at a stroke, makes McCarthy a contender for the Nobel Prize for Literature.’ Andrew O’Hagan ‘ The Road is about tenderness despite everything. McCarthy has unmade the world, but against all the odds, this novel holds out a thin strand of hope: for the power of language and storytelling even at the end of all things’ Financial Times ‘McCarthy conjures from this pitiless flight the miracle of unswerving humanity. Gripping beyond belief.’ Chris Cleave, Sunday Telegraph ‘So good that it will devour you. It is incandescent.’ Daily Telegraph ‘Trenchant and terrifying, written with stripped-down urgency and fuelled by the force of a universal nightmare . . . This is art that both frightens and inspires’ New York Times ‘You will read on, absolutely convinced, thrilled, mesmerized. All the modern novel can do is done here.’ Alan Warner, Guardian ‘A masterpiece that will soon be considered a classic.’ Herald ‘We are watching a late flowering of a great American novelist. For all its grim imaginings, The Road ’s divine language carries its two entwined souls above the darkness. McCarthy continues to carry the fire’ Independent on Sunday ‘A spectacular novel . . . Images continue to recur long after it has been put aside’ Scotland on Sunday ‘A gripping, heart-rending human story, which explores the depths of despair and savagery beside the heights of love, tenderness and self-sacrifice. McCarthy has delivered a master stroke: this tale is beautiful, hypnotic, and terrifyingly real’ New Statesman ‘No novelist working today is better suited to chronicle the end of the world than Cormac McCarthy . . . What really elevates the novel from its slough of despond is the sheer, terrible beauty of the writing’ Sunday Times ‘McCarthy shows that he is one of the greatest American writers alive’ Times Literary Supplement THE ROAD Cormac McCarthy is the author of twelve acclaimed novels. Among his honours are the National Book Award, the National Book Critics Circle Award and the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction. IN 2022, A GLOBAL LITERARY EVENT: Cormac McCarthy’s first novels in more than a decade STELLA MARIS and THE PASSENGER The Passenger is the story of a salvage diver, haunted by loss, afraid of the watery deep, pursued for a conspiracy beyond his understanding, and longing for a death he cannot reconcile with God. Stella Maris is the story of a mathematician, twenty years old, admitted to the hospital with forty thousand dollars in a plastic bag and one request: She does not want to talk about her brother. Set eight years apart, this remarkable interconnected story of two siblings is a new two-book masterpiece from a giant of American literature, one that challenges our notions of morality, faith, truth, and human existence. Available in hardback, eBook, trade paperback and digital audio formats Autumn 2022 Also available as a limited edition slipcase boxed set ALL THE PRETTY HORSES Cormac McCarthy Scars have the strange power to remind us that our past is real. John Grady Cole is the last bewildered survivor of long generations of Texas ranchers. Finding himself cut off from the only life he has ever wanted, he sets out for Mexico with his friend Lacey Rawlins. Befriending a third boy on the way, they find a country beyond their imagining: barren and beautiful, rugged yet cruelly civilized; a place where dreams are paid for in blood. The first volume in McCarthy’s legendary Border Trilogy, All the Pretty Horses is an acknowledged masterpiece and a grand love story: steeped in the wisdom that comes only from loss, it is a magnificent parable of responsibility, revenge and survival. THE CROSSING Cormac McCarthy Life is a memory, and then it is nothing. Set on the south-western ranches in the years before the Second World War, Cormac McCarthy’s The Crossing follows the fortunes of sixteen-year-old Billy Parham and his younger brother Boyd.
